owasp_top10: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
cwe: CWE-345 - Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity

threat.title: Webhook Replay Attack via Missing Timestamp and Signature Validation
threat.description: When webhook endpoints lack proper timestamp validation, signature verification, and idempotency controls, attackers can capture legitimate webhook payloads and replay them multiple times. This enables fraudulent payment processing, duplicate order fulfillment, unauthorized access grants, and accounting manipulation. Webhook replay attacks are particularly devastating in payment systems where a single captured 'payment.succeeded' webhook can be replayed to credit accounts multiple times without actual payment.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker intercepts legitimate Stripe 'payment_intent.succeeded' webhook via compromised proxy or network sniffing
2. Extracts webhook payload containing $500 payment confirmation for Order #12345
3. Bypasses signature validation due to missing or weak HMAC verification
4. Replays the captured webhook 10 times over 24 hours to the victim's endpoint
5. Victim's application processes each replay, crediting user account with $500 ten times ($5,000 total)
6. Attacker withdraws funds or exploits duplicated credits before detection, leaving merchant with $4,500 loss
prerequisites: Webhook endpoint lacks timestamp validation (no expiry check)
Missing or improperly implemented HMAC signature verification
No idempotency key tracking to prevent duplicate processing
No rate limiting on webhook endpoint
Application doesn't verify event uniqueness with payment provider API

cve_references.description: Waitress HTTP Server - Request smuggling allowing webhook replay attacks by manipulating Transfer-Encoding headers
cve_references.affected_systems: Python web applications using Waitress server

cve_references.description: GitHub webhook signature validation bypass via timing attack in Ruby implementations
Adminer - SSRF enabling webhook payload capture and replay through database server
cve_references.affected_systems: Applications using Adminer for database management

real_world_breaches.incident: Stripe Webhook Replay Attack on E-commerce Platform
real_world_breaches.impact: Payment processor reported merchant losses of $120,000 due to replayed payment confirmation webhooks. Attackers captured legitimate webhooks and replayed them to credit accounts 50+ times.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Missing timestamp validation and no idempotency key tracking in webhook handler

real_world_breaches.impact: Multiple merchants affected with total losses exceeding $250,000. Attackers replayed IPN (Instant Payment Notification) messages to trigger duplicate order fulfillment.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Insufficient validation of payment notification authenticity and lack of transaction ID deduplication

real_world_breaches.impact: Vulnerability in webhook signature validation allowed attackers to forge GitHub events, triggering unauthorized CI/CD deployments
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Timing attack in HMAC comparison and missing X-Hub-Signature-256 validation

real_world_breaches.impact: Bug bounty report disclosed replay attack vector affecting Shopify apps, enabling duplicate order creation and inventory manipulation
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Webhook endpoints using HTTP instead of HTTPS, enabling MITM capture and replay

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Stripe webhook replay attack allowing unlimited account credits due to missing timestamp validation and idempotency controls
PayPal IPN replay vulnerability enabling duplicate payment confirmation and order fulfillment
Slack webhook signature bypass via timing attack in HMAC comparison, allowing forged event injection

security_research.title: Webhook Security Best Practices
security_research.key_finding: 75% of webhook implementations lack proper signature validation, and 90% don't implement timestamp checks or idempotency controls

security_research.title: OWASP API Security Top 10 - API8:2023 Security Misconfiguration
security_research.key_finding: Webhook endpoints frequently lack authentication, enabling replay and forgery attacks

security_research.title: Payment Webhook Replay Attacks: A Systematic Analysis
security_research.key_finding: 84% of tested e-commerce platforms vulnerable to webhook replay, with average window of 48+ hours for successful replay

mitigations.title: Implement Stripe-Style Webhook Signature Validation with Timestamp Verification
mitigations.description: Validate webhook authenticity using HMAC-SHA256 signature verification combined with timestamp checks to prevent replay attacks. This is the industry-standard approach used by Stripe, GitHub, and Slack.
nist_csf: PR.AC-7 - Users and devices are authenticated
validation_steps: Test webhook with valid signature and timestamp (should succeed)
Test with valid signature but old timestamp >5min (should fail)
Test with invalid signature (should fail)
Test replaying same event ID twice (second should be marked duplicate)
Verify Redis/cache stores processed event IDs with 24h TTL
Use stripe CLI for testing: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/webhooks/stripe

mitigations.title: GitHub Webhook Signature Validation with SHA-256
mitigations.description: Implement GitHub's webhook signature validation using X-Hub-Signature-256 header with constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks.
nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify software
validation_steps: Generate webhook secret in GitHub repository settings
Test with GitHub webhook deliveries page (redeliver feature)
Verify X-Hub-Signature-256 validation (not just X-Hub-Signature)
Test replay protection using same X-GitHub-Delivery ID
Monitor webhook delivery failures in GitHub UI

mitigations.title: Spring Boot Webhook Signature Validation with Rate Limiting
mitigations.description: Enterprise-grade webhook validation for Java/Spring Boot applications with built-in rate limiting and distributed idempotency tracking.
nist_csf: PR.AC-7 - Users and devices are authenticated
DE.CM-7 - Monitoring for unauthorized personnel access
validation_steps: Add Stripe Java SDK dependency: com.stripe:stripe-java:24.+
Configure Redis connection in application.yml
Test with Stripe CLI: stripe trigger payment_intent.succeeded
Verify timestamp validation rejects old events
Confirm Redis stores processed event IDs with 24h expiry
Load test with JMeter to verify rate limiting

mitigations.title: Flask Webhook Validation with Azure Monitor Integration
mitigations.description: Python/Flask webhook implementation with comprehensive logging, signature validation, and Azure Monitor integration for threat detection.
nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated
validation_steps: Install dependencies: pip install flask redis opencensus-ext-azure
Configure Azure Application Insights connection string
Test with Stripe CLI: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:5000/webhooks/stripe
Verify logs appear in Azure Monitor Logs workspace
Test replay attack detection by resending same event
Create Azure Monitor alert for 'Invalid webhook signature' events

mitigations.title: Implement Webhook URL Obfuscation and Rate Limiting
mitigations.description: Use unpredictable webhook URLs and implement aggressive rate limiting to prevent enumeration and brute-force replay attacks.
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Generate webhook token: node -e "console.log(require('crypto').randomBytes(32).toString('hex'))"
Register obfuscated URL in Stripe dashboard
Test rate limiting with ab: ab -n 200 -c 10 https://api.example.com/webhooks/...
Verify 404 response for /webhooks/stripe (without token)
Monitor Azure Front Door metrics for blocked requests
Implement IP whitelist for known provider IPs (optional)

indicators: Multiple webhook events with identical event IDs within short time period
Webhook requests with timestamps older than 5 minutes
Signature validation failures from unexpected IP addresses
Duplicate payment confirmations for same transaction ID
Webhook requests to /webhooks/* without proper token/signature headers
Unusual spike in webhook volume from single IP address
Webhook events triggering duplicate database inserts (constraint violations)
Payment events not matching Stripe API verification
Webhook requests during off-hours or outside normal traffic patterns

log_queries.query:
// Detect webhook replay attacks via duplicate event IDs
AppTraces
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where Message contains "webhook" or Message contains "stripe"
| extend EventId = extract(@'event_id[":]\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)', 1, Message)
| where isnotempty(EventId)
| summarize RequestCount = count(), 
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), 
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated), 
            SourceIPs = make_set(ClientIP) 
  by EventId
| where RequestCount > 1  // Duplicate processing
| where datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen) < 60  // Within 1 hour
| project EventId, RequestCount, FirstSeen, LastSeen, TimeDiff_Minutes = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen), SourceIPs
| order by RequestCount desc

// Detect webhook signature validation failures
AppExceptions
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where OuterMessage contains "Invalid webhook signature" or OuterMessage contains "Webhook timestamp too old"
| summarize FailureCount = count(), 
            DistinctIPs = dcount(ClientIP),
            IPs = make_set(ClientIP, 10) 
  by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), OuterMessage
| where FailureCount > 5  // More than 5 failures in 5 minutes
| project TimeGenerated, OuterMessage, FailureCount, DistinctIPs, IPs
| order by TimeGenerated desc

index=app_logs sourcetype=webhook
| rex field=_raw "event_id[\":]\s*(?<event_id>[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)"
| stats count as request_count, 
        earliest(_time) as first_seen, 
        latest(_time) as last_seen, 
        values(src_ip) as source_ips 
  by event_id
| where request_count > 1
| eval time_diff_minutes = round((last_seen - first_seen) / 60, 2)
| where time_diff_minutes < 60
| table event_id, request_count, first_seen, last_seen, time_diff_minutes, source_ips
| sort - request_count

index=app_logs sourcetype=webhook ("Invalid webhook signature" OR "Webhook timestamp too old")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as failure_count, 
        dc(src_ip) as distinct_ips, 
        values(src_ip) as source_ips 
  by _time, log_level, message
| where failure_count > 5
| table _time, message, failure_count, distinct_ips, source_ips
| sort - _time

event.category: "web" AND 
  (message: "Invalid webhook signature" OR message: "Webhook timestamp too old") AND 
  @timestamp >= "now-1h"
| stats count() by source.ip, event.outcome
| where count > 5
| sort count desc

fields @timestamp, @message, requestParameters.eventId, sourceIPAddress
| filter @message like /webhook/
| filter @message like /duplicate/ or @message like /replay/
| stats count(*) as request_count by requestParameters.eventId, sourceIPAddress
| filter request_count > 1
| sort request_count desc

tools.name: Azure Sentinel Workbook
tools.usage: Create workbook with queries above to visualize webhook replay attacks, signature failures, and duplicate event processing

tools.name: Splunk Security Essentials
tools.usage: Configure baseline for normal webhook volume and alert on >3x standard deviation

tools.name: Elastic SIEM
tools.usage: Create detection rule: 5+ signature failures from same IP in 5 minutes = High severity alert
